title: cyathella corymbosa
intro: Cyathella corymbosa, also known as Marsh Cinquefoil or Marsh Five-Finger, is a perennial plant species native to wet meadows and marshes in Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, and its extracts have been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its potential health benefits. Traditionally, Cyathella corymbosa is prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and stems are used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments.
